latest.title: Snakes can sense heat when light disappears
latest.script_key: snakes can sense heat when light disappears watch the head before the strike because some snakes have heat sensitive pits that detect warm bodies that gives them a second kind of vision at night the target glows to them without glowing to us which reptile sense next

latest.title: Dogs read human pointing surprisingly well
latest.script_key: dogs can follow a human point fast watch the eyes check the person because dogs are unusually good at using human gestures that skill helps them cooperate with us before any command gets repeated the small glance is social intelligence not random obedience what dog skill should come next

latest.title: Bull sharks do something you never expected
latest.script_key: here s the part nobody tells you bull sharks in fiji don t hunt alone watch this one twice see the same two sharks cruising the shipwreck again and again they re not just swimming they re keeping the same schedules the same partners for years that s why researchers think they re making friends

latest.title: Bats play the best cricket match you can't see
latest.script_key: watch this bat score its mouth stays closed ears twitch like radar dishes that s because it s not hearing the cricket it s screaming at 100 decibels and listening to the echo the sound bounces off the cricket s wings mid flight so it knows exactly where to swoop that s why the catch looks impossible

latest.title: Trees don’t just drop seeds they shoot them
latest.script_key: watch this one twice see those little pods flipping in the breeze that s not wind that s the tree itself hurling seeds the pods dry out and snap like a tiny catapult because the canopy s crowded they shoot sideways to land in gaps where sunlight hits the ground

latest.title: Coral reefs are cities built by tiny animals
latest.script_key: coral reefs are built by tiny living builders watch the reef texture because coral animals build hard skeletons that stack into habitat fish algae and countless small creatures then use that structure like a city the color sits on top of engineering which habitat next

latest.title: Halloween bats hate the dark myth
latest.script_key: bats don t hate the dark it s the echoes they hate that s why they only fly out when it s properly lit so they can hear their own squeaks bounce back watch this one twice their ears twitch to the millisecond now tell me your favorite halloween costume fail

latest.title: Mountains aren’t forever snowcapped but always changing
latest.script_key: even the tallest peaks lie those snowcaps aren t just decoration they re a time bomb glaciers chew rock away faster than the mountain grows i love this one watch how the ice grinds the ridge down like a cheese grater that s why peaks we think are ancient are really getting shorter every year

latest.title: How dogs cool down without sweating like us before the obvious moment

latest.title: Butterflies can taste a flower by landing on it

latest.title: Camels survive Sahara without water for weeks
latest.script_key: camels don t store water in their humps they store fat their blood holds way more water than yours that s why they can go days without drinking watch their noses they can close to block sand and keep moisture in so what would you do if you had blood like that

latest.title: bats hunt like cricket captains
latest.script_key: crickets think they re safe in daylight that s why i love this one watch the bat s ears twitch like radar dishes it s not guessing the crickets own wing beats echo back loud and clear because that s the frequency the bat locked onto so yeah the bat s basically listening to the crickets heartbeat
